commands.command: python3 runtime-probe (service registry + scanner summary + metadata/alert identity)
commands.key_output: service_ids include schema_migrator/account_stash_harvester; stale scanner summary still returns status=ok; strategy metadata keys not referenced in runtime; alert id formula is concat(scanner_run_id,'-',item_or_market_key)

observations.details: Harvester startup resolves cursor from ClickHouse checkpoint and optional cursor-file override via _resolve_start_cursor(); stale cursor equality is handled as non-advancing status 'stale cursor'.
Duplicate stash ids within a single payload page are deduplicated before writes.
429 handling pauses queue polling using Retry-After (or fallback), writes bronze_requests, and reports status=rate_limited.
Service action API supports start/stop/restart only for controllable services, but semantic health classification in snapshots can mislead dashboard operators.

defect_clusters.summary: Service snapshots always include optional and one-shot services, then dashboard/frontend denominator uses the full list, distorting operational signal.
defect_clusters.impact: Homepage 'services running' can look unhealthy even when core always-on trading path is healthy.

defect_clusters.summary: scanner_summary_payload returns status='ok' for any non-empty table regardless of last run age; no stale/degraded inference.
defect_clusters.impact: Operator may infer scanner is currently healthy while data is stale.

notes: Evidence-only delivery due multiple independent product/runtime defects; no single isolated defect cluster was remediated in this task.
